message:
feat(atc): add VoI control, identifiability debt, and safe experiment design (br-0qt6e.3.14)

New atc_voi_control.rs module implementing:
- IdentifiabilityDebt ledger: tracks where evidence is insufficient for
  confident adaptation, certification, or attribution. 6 debt types
  (sparse, confounded, regime-discounted, contaminated, weak-counterfactual,
  fairness-bound)
- VoI scorer: estimates expected learning value of experiments against
  noise, fairness, and tail-risk costs. 7 decision outcomes including
  "do not experiment" as first-class safe outcome
- Experiment eligibility gates: 8-gate check (risk level, regime stability,
  safe mode, evidence quality, decision-sufficiency, budget, fairness, VoI)
- Experiment budget tables: per-family per-cohort per-window limits with
  global 5% experiment fraction cap
- ExperimentRecord: links experiments to experience ledger with dual-purpose
  tracking (control + information value)
- 25 passing unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","title":"ATC Learning 3.11: fairness, anti-starvation, and per-agent/project harm budgets","description":"# ATC Learning 3.11: Fairness, anti-starvation, and per-agent/project harm budgets

## Why
A globally efficient ATC can still be user-hostile if it repeatedly burdens the same agent, project, or cohort because those cases are easier to measure, easier to optimize against, or more visible in the telemetry. The learning stack needs an explicit fairness and anti-starvation contract so it does not concentrate probes, advisories, suppressions, false positives, or intervention friction onto the same targets while claiming aggregate utility gains.

## Deliverables
- define the fairness observables ATC should track across agents, projects, and other relevant cohorts: intervention frequency, false-action exposure, suppression exposure, unresolved-backlog burden, probe burden, and operator-noise concentration
- define anti-starvation rules so quiet, low-volume, or low-visibility agents/projects are not systematically ignored when they actually need attention
- define per-agent, per-project, or per-cohort harm budgets and concentration limits that constrain both act-time decisions and policy adaptation
- define the interaction between global expected utility and fairness constraints, including when a fairness or anti-starvation guard vetoes an otherwise attractive action or policy move
- define machine-readable fairness debt, burden-concentration, and most-impacted-target indicators that can flow into summaries, audits, and rollout checks
- define how fairness state decays, resets, or is reweighted across regime changes so old concentration does not either vanish too fast or poison the future forever
- document what forms of asymmetry are acceptable, what forms are suspicious, and what evidence would justify deliberate unequal treatment

## Acceptance Criteria
- no agent, project, or tracked cohort can absorb an unbounded or poorly explained share of probes, advisories, suppressions, or false-action harm simply because it is easier for the learner to optimize there
- the fairness contract is explicit enough that later surfaces, audits, and tests can distinguish healthy prioritization from harmful concentration or starvation
- the design states what happens when fairness constraints conflict with short-horizon global utility, including which side wins and how that decision is made visible
- future verification beads can derive anti-starvation, bounded-concentration, and fairness-budget tests from this bead alone without chat archaeology

## Important Notes
This bead is not about flattening all distinctions or banning prioritization. It is about preventing a mathematically sophisticated system from becoming quietly unfair, annoying, or trust-eroding to the exact users it is supposed to help.","notes":"Implemented fairness, anti-starvation, and harm-budget contract in atc_fairness.rs;  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","title":"ATC Learning 3.12: feedback-loop contamination detection, anti-gaming safeguards, and adversarial robustness","description":"# ATC Learning 3.12: Feedback-loop contamination detection, anti-gaming safeguards, and adversarial robustness

## Why
ATC is not learning from a neutral laboratory. It learns from agents, operators, and systems that can respond strategically or accidentally distort the evidence stream. Interventions can change observability, agents can learn to look alive without being healthy, noisy cohorts can dominate labels, and duplicated/delayed/replayed signals can make the learner over-trust bad evidence. Without an explicit anti-gaming and contamination contract, the rest of the math can still be elegant while the system learns the wrong lesson.

## Deliverables
- define the contamination and strategic-behavior threat model for ATC learning: replayed or duplicated events, spoofed liveness, performative acknowledgements, message floods, reservation churn, operator-induced selective labeling, coordinated agent behavior, intervention-caused observability shifts, and other self-fulfilling or self-canceling feedback loops
- define the observables and machine-readable suspicion states for evidence quality at the experience, actor, cohort, policy, and regime levels
- define how suspicious evidence is downweighted, quarantined, capped in influence, excluded from promotion evidence, or retained only for monitoring rather than control
- define robust aggregation or influence-limiting rules so a small number of adversarial or low-quality traces cannot dominate policy updates, fairness state, or previews
- define how contamination suspicion affects admissibility gates, certificates, previews, summaries, fairness logic, rollback triggers, and operator-visible reason codes
- define how ATC distinguishes real improvement from intervention-shaped observability artifacts, label leakage, or strategic adaptation by the agents it supervises
- define recovery, decay, and re-entry rules so contaminated evidence does not poison the future forever but also cannot be forgotten instantly without explanation
- define how audit artifacts and operator surfaces expose "data not trusted" conditions without collapsing into paranoia or noisy false alarms
- define efficiency constraints so robustness checks use maintained statistics, bounded state, and targeted diagnostics instead of expensive full-history rescans

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly prevents policy promotion, preview trust, or unsafe action confidence from silently relying on obviously suspicious or strategically contaminated evidence
- future surfaces and tests can explain when ATC is acting on trusted evidence, when it is monitoring but not trusting certain data, and why that happened
- the robustness contract is specific enough that unit tests, E2E scenarios, audits, and rollout gates can derive anti-gaming and contamination cases from this bead alone without chat archaeology
- the bead strengthens the learning system without turning it into opaque paranoia theater or unbounded forensic overhead

## Important Notes
This bead is not optional hardening polish. It is a core requirement for any autonomous learning loop that operates in a strategic environment and supervises actors that can react to the system itself.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:54:50.696976001Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:40:30.312811097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:40:30.312161992Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_contamination.rs: EvidenceQuality enum (Trusted/Suspect/Quarantined/Capped) with weight multipliers. 9 ContaminationKind types (DuplicateTrace, EventBurst, SpoofedLiveness, PerformativeAck, ReservationChurn, InfluenceDomination, ObservabilityArtifact, OperatorIntervention, CoordinatedBehavior).  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","title":"ATC Learning 3.13: interference-aware credit assignment, overlapping intervention attribution, and causal eligibility windows","description":"# ATC Learning 3.13: Interference-aware credit assignment, overlapping intervention attribution, and causal eligibility windows

## Why
ATC does not operate in a clean single-cause world. Multiple interventions can overlap in time, operators can change controls mid-stream, exogenous project events can resolve a problem naturally, and one action can change the observability of another. If the learner credits or blames the wrong cause, it can look mathematically disciplined while adapting in the wrong direction. This bead exists to make attribution itself first-class, rather than assuming every resolved outcome belongs cleanly to one decision.

## Deliverables
- define the interference model for ATC learning: simultaneous or overlapping interventions, operator control changes, natural recoveries, exogenous load or coordination shifts, and observation-only events that occur inside an attribution window
- define causal eligibility windows and candidate-cause sets so the system knows which open experiences are even eligible to receive credit or blame for a given downstream outcome
- define how overlapping eligible causes are handled: downgraded confidence, censoring, explicit ambiguity states, bounded multi-touch credit, or "do not learn from this" outcomes when identifiability is too weak
- define how attribution confidence or confoundedness is represented in experience rows, rollups, summaries, previews, certificates, and audit artifacts
- define how interference-aware attribution interacts with contamination detection, fairness/burden accounting, regime changes, and rollout decisions so overlapping causes do not silently distort other guardrails
- define efficient runtime structures for maintaining the open-experience frontier per target or cohort, candidate-cause lookup, and attribution resolution without full-history rescans or unbounded combinatorial blowups
- define how the system distinguishes true improvements from natural recovery, parallel operator intervention, or competing ATC actions that would otherwise produce false learning signals
- define operator-visible reason codes and explainability hooks for "improved but attribution uncertain", "confounded by concurrent change", and "insufficient causal isolation to update policy"

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly prevents ATC from treating every resolved outcome as a clean label when overlapping causes or exogenous changes make attribution ambiguous
- later math, surface, and verification beads can explain when ATC learned from an outcome, when it refused to learn, and why attribution confidence was downgraded
- the attribution contract is strong enough that summaries, previews, audit artifacts, unit tests, E2E scripts, and rollout reviews can all reuse it without inventing incompatible causal stories
- the design keeps attribution honest without exploding runtime cost, history scans, or operator cognitive overhead

## Important Notes
This bead is the causal honesty layer for the learning loop. It should make the system more trustworthy to users by teaching it to say "multiple things happened; I cannot honestly assign credit yet" instead of silently learning the wrong lesson.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:44:27.884027330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:29:57.638511803Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:29:57.638019131Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_attribution.rs: AttributionConfidence enum (Clean/Primary/Shared/Ambiguous/Censored) with learning_weight (1.0/0.8/0.5/0/0). attribute_outcome() algorithm: 1) filter by eligibility window, 2) single=Clean, 3) disjoint subsystems=Clean, 4) dominant recency (2x)=Primary, 5) close overlap=Shared. -{"id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","title":"ATC Learning 3.14: safe value-of-information control, identifiability debt, and targeted low-risk experiment design","description":"# ATC Learning 3.14: Safe value-of-information control, identifiability debt, and targeted low-risk experiment design

## Why
Even with strong logging, certificates, contamination defenses, and interference-aware attribution, ATC can still get stuck in uncertainty-limited regimes where the system cannot safely learn enough from ambient production behavior alone. The smartest next step is to make ATC an active but disciplined scientist: it should know when uncertainty, confounding, or sparse support is blocking improvement, maintain an explicit identifiability-debt ledger, and spend tiny, low-risk, budgeted experiments or probes only when the expected information value clearly beats the user-noise and safety cost.

## Deliverables
- define an identifiability-debt ledger for action families, cohorts, and policy questions where current evidence is insufficient for confident adaptation, certification, or attribution
- define a safe value-of-information or knowledge-gradient scoring layer that estimates the expected learning value of a candidate probe, advisory variant, randomized tie-break, or low-risk intervention against its user-noise, fairness, contamination, and tail-risk cost
- define experiment eligibility gates so only low-risk, reversibly bounded actions can ever be chosen for information gain, and only when the normal policy is uncertainty-limited rather than already decision-sufficient
- define experiment budget tables across action family, cohort, regime, and time window so learning pressure cannot silently grow into spam, unfair burden concentration, or operational chaos
- define how targeted experiments are announced, previewed, logged, and linked to the experience ledger so later attribution, certificates, and audits know that an action had both control value and information value
- define how active experiments interact with contamination defense, interference-aware attribution, fairness budgets, and rollback so the system never learns aggressively from suspicious or strategically distorted settings
- define when the correct decision is "do not experiment" because the regime is unstable, the evidence is contaminated, the likely user burden is too high, or the incremental information value is too small
- define compact runtime artifacts for the whole stack: identifiability-debt tables, value-of-information scores, experiment-eligibility automata, budgeted experiment scheduler, and machine-readable reason codes for why an information-seeking action was allowed or refused

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly upgrades ATC from passive learning only to safe active information acquisition, but only inside a strict low-risk and low-noise envelope
- later surfaces and tests can explain when a probe or intervention was issued partly to reduce uncertainty, what debt it was meant to repay, and why that was worth the cost
- the design makes "do not experiment" a first-class safe outcome when information value, fairness, contamination, or operator burden do not justify action
- the new learning mechanism is specific enough that summaries, previews, explainability cards, E2E scripts, and rollout gates can all reuse it without inventing incompatible stories

## Important Notes
This bead is the dual-control / active-experimentation layer for ATC. It is radically accretive because it lets the system learn faster and more honestly under sparse or confounded evidence, while still staying bounded, auditable, and  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.2","title":"ATC Learning 3.2: materialized sufficient statistics and EWMA/delay rollups","description":"# ATC Learning 3.2: Materialized sufficient statistics and EWMA/delay rollups

## Why
The learning loop cannot afford to rescan raw history. It needs maintained statistics that summarize outcomes by the dimensions that matter.

## Deliverables
- define the per-stratum, per-action, per-policy rollups needed for learning
- maintain resolved and open counts, realized-loss aggregates, EWMA loss, and delay histograms
- define update semantics when rows resolve or are censored
- make the summaries cheap to query from the operator loop and robot or TUI surfaces
- document which statistics are authoritative and which are derived caches

## Acceptance Criteria
- maintained statistics are sufficient for learning, summaries, and verification without raw-history rescans in steady state
- update semantics are explicit enough that later work can reason about resolve, censor, and delay effects without inventing side rules
- authoritative-versus-derived boundaries are clear enough to prevent operator surfaces and math components from reading incompatible states

## Important Notes
This bead exists to keep the adaptive loop fast and explainable at the same time.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:56:43.865397964Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:13:49.033864727Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:13:49.033346186Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.3","title":"ATC Learning 3.3: empirical-Bayes shrinkage across agent, project, and program strata","description":"# ATC Learning 3.3: Empirical-Bayes shrinkage across agent, project, and program strata

## Why
Many ATC buckets will be sparse. If the system trusts tiny sample counts too aggressively, it will overfit and make unstable policy moves.

## Deliverables
- define the cohort hierarchy used for shrinkage: global, program, project, agent, and action-family strata
- choose the shrinkage model for success/failure and realized-loss estimates
- define minimum-support behavior and how posterior estimates back off to broader cohorts
- expose the effective sample size or strength-of-evidence concept for inspection
- document when shrinkage should be disabled or reduced under clear high-support evidence

## Acceptance Criteria
- sparse buckets cannot produce aggressive policy movement without explicit support and visible fallback to broader cohorts
- the shrinkage hierarchy is concrete enough that learning, summaries, and tests can all agree on where evidence is borrowed from
- operators and verification suites can inspect effective sample size or equivalent evidence strength rather than treating shrinkage as hidden magic

## Important Notes
Shrinkage is here to prevent unstable behavior, not to hide uncertainty. The system should stay conservative when evidence is thin.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:56:52.594118260Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:21:47.015946127Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:21:47.013512589Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_shrinkage.rs implements empirical-Bayes shrinkage with 4-level cohort hierarchy (Global/Program/Project/Agent).  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","title":"ATC Learning 3.4: conformal risk control and per-action false-action budget tables","description":"# ATC Learning 3.4: Conformal risk control and per-action false-action budget tables

## Why
ATC needs a mathematically explicit brake on harmful actions. Learning is only useful if false positives stay bounded for each action family and operating stratum.

## Deliverables
- define the stratum-aware conformal or CRC budget tables ATC will maintain
- define the nonconformity scores used for each action family
- define bucket starvation and merge fallback behavior
- define budget-consumption, cooldown, and recovery semantics so operators and tests can tell whether action is blocked because evidence is weak, a budget is exhausted, or the system is intentionally cooling down
- define how risk budgets gate action admissibility and exploration
- expose operator-visible health indicators and reason categories when a budget is healthy, stressed, cooling down, or actively blocking action

## Acceptance Criteria
- the risk tables define explicit false-action budgets by action family and stratum rather than vague "be conservative" guidance
- starvation and merge fallback rules are explicit enough that sparse buckets cannot silently widen risk beyond the intended safety envelope
- operator and verification beads can tell when a budget is healthy, stressed, cooling down, or blocking action from this bead alone
- suppression due to risk budgets is explainable enough that downstream surfaces do not collapse it into generic silence

## Important Notes
This bead is the explicit brake pedal for adaptive ATC. If it is underspecified, the rest of the math becomes unsafe.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:57:01.351634185Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:23:29.188361019Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:23:29.187840494Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_risk_budgets.rs (809 lines, 16 tests): per-stratum CRC budget tables with BudgetHealth state machine (Healthy/Stressed/CoolingDown/Blocking).  {"id":"br-aazao.11","title":"Verification Coverage 4.4: failure, recovery, stress, and performance E2E matrix completion","description":"# Verification Coverage 4.4: Failure, Recovery, Stress, and Performance E2E Matrix Completion

## Why
A system can look well-tested while still being dangerously under-tested in the exact scenarios that matter most operationally: crash/restart, migration, corruption, fault injection, load spikes, rate limiting, soak behavior, and performance regressions.

## Deliverables
- define and close the failure/recovery matrix for:
  - crash/restart and process death
  - DB corruption and migration paths
  - fault injection and failure injection
  - guard enforcement and conflict storms
  - load / stress / soak / perf-regression lanes
  - rate limiting and overload behavior
- ensure those suites produce detailed artifacts rather than just pass/fail shells
- define which scenarios should remain deterministic and which can be probabilistic but bounded

## Acceptance Criteria
- the repo has explicit operational confidence rather than only happy-path confidence
- failure/recovery suites are good enough to debug from first failure artifacts
- performance-related suites are tied to thresholds and reproducible enough to be actionable

## Important Notes
This bead exists to prevent “we have lots of tests” from meaning “we only test success.”","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:52:55.643042073Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:57:56.896186903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","failure-recovery","performance","stress","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.11","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:52:55.643042073Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.11","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:57:35.269614163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.11","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:57:46.065005857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.11","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:57:56.894713780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+//! Safe value-of-information control, identifiability debt, and targeted
+//! low-risk experiment design (br-0qt6e.3.14).
+//!
+//! Upgrades ATC from passive learning to safe active information acquisition
+//! within a strict low-risk and low-noise envelope. The system knows when
+//! uncertainty is blocking improvement, maintains an explicit identifiability-debt
+//! ledger, and spends tiny, budgeted experiments only when expected information
+//! value clearly beats user-noise and safety cost.
+//!
+//! # Design Principle
+//!
+//! "Do not experiment" is a first-class safe outcome. Active information
+//! acquisition is the exception, not the rule, and is only justified when:
+//! 1. The normal policy is uncertainty-limited (not already decision-sufficient)
+//! 2. The candidate experiment is low-risk and reversibly bounded
+//! 3. The expected information value exceeds the user-noise cost
+//! 4. The experiment fits within fairness, contamination, and budget constraints
+//!
+//! # Architecture
+//!
+//! ```text
+//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+//! │  Identifiability-Debt Ledger                                    │
+//! │  - Tracks where evidence is insufficient for confident          │
+//! │    adaptation, certification, or attribution                    │
+//! │  - Per action family, cohort, policy question                   │
+//! ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+//! │  Value-of-Information Scorer                                    │
+//! │  - Estimates expected learning value of candidate experiments   │
+//! │  - Compares against user-noise, fairness, and tail-risk cost   │
+//! ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+//! │  Experiment Eligibility Gates                                   │
+//! │  - Only low-risk, reversible actions qualify                    │
+//! │  - Only when normal policy is uncertainty-limited               │
+//! ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+//! │  Experiment Budget Tables                                       │
+//! │  - Per action family, cohort, regime, time window               │
+//! │  - Prevents learning pressure from becoming spam                │
+//! ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+//! │  Experiment Logger                                              │
+//! │  - Links experiments to experience ledger                       │
+//! │  - Announces, previews, and logs information-seeking actions    │
+//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+//! ```
+
+#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
+
+use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
+
+use crate::experience::EffectKind;
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Constants
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Minimum observations before an action family can be considered
+/// "decision-sufficient" (not needing active experiments).
+pub const DECISION_SUFFICIENT_OBS: u64 = 100;
+
+/// Minimum confidence level (1 - uncertainty) before experiments stop.
+pub const DECISION_SUFFICIENT_CONFIDENCE: f64 = 0.80;
+
+/// Maximum fraction of total actions that can be experiments (global cap).
+pub const MAX_EXPERIMENT_FRACTION_GLOBAL: f64 = 0.05; // 5%
+
+/// Maximum experiments per agent per hour.
+pub const MAX_EXPERIMENTS_PER_AGENT_PER_HOUR: u32 = 3;
+
+/// Maximum experiments per cohort per hour.
+pub const MAX_EXPERIMENTS_PER_COHORT_PER_HOUR: u32 = 10;
+
+/// Minimum value-of-information score before an experiment is justified.
+/// Below this, "do not experiment" wins.
+pub const MIN_VOI_SCORE: f64 = 0.10;
+
+/// Maximum user-noise cost multiplier. An experiment whose noise cost
+/// exceeds this fraction of its VoI is refused.
+pub const MAX_NOISE_TO_VOI_RATIO: f64 = 0.50;
+
+/// Default experiment validity window (microseconds). After this,
+/// the experiment budget entry expires and must be re-evaluated.
+pub const DEFAULT_EXPERIMENT_WINDOW_MICROS: i64 = 3_600_000_000; // 1 hour
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Identifiability debt
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Type of identifiability gap.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum DebtType {
+    /// Too few observations for reliable estimation.
+    SparseData,
+    /// Observations exist but are confounded by overlapping interventions.
+    ConfoundedAttribution,
+    /// Regime changed and old evidence was discounted.
+    RegimeDiscounted,
+    /// Evidence is contaminated (high suspect/quarantined fraction).
+    ContaminatedEvidence,
+    /// Policy comparison is uncertain because the candidate was rarely selected.
+    WeakCounterfactual,
+    /// Fairness constraints prevent learning from certain cohorts.
+    FairnessBound,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for DebtType {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::SparseData => write!(f, "sparse_data"),
+            Self::ConfoundedAttribution => write!(f, "confounded_attribution"),
+            Self::RegimeDiscounted => write!(f, "regime_discounted"),
+            Self::ContaminatedEvidence => write!(f, "contaminated_evidence"),
+            Self::WeakCounterfactual => write!(f, "weak_counterfactual"),
+            Self::FairnessBound => write!(f, "fairness_bound"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// A single entry in the identifiability-debt ledger.
+///
+/// Each entry tracks where ATC's evidence is insufficient for confident
+/// adaptation, certification, or attribution.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct IdentifiabilityDebt {
+    /// Unique debt identifier.
+    pub debt_id: u64,
+    /// Which action family is affected.
+    pub action_family: String,
+    /// Which cohort/stratum is affected (e.g., "claude-code:project-x:advisory").
+    pub cohort_key: String,
+    /// What kind of identifiability gap exists.
+    pub debt_type: DebtType,
+    /// Current severity (0.0 = minor gap, 1.0 = completely blind).
+    pub severity: f64,
+    /// How many observations exist for this stratum.
+    pub observation_count: u64,
+    /// How many are needed for decision-sufficiency.
+    pub required_count: u64,
+    /// Current confidence level (0.0-1.0).
+    pub current_confidence: f64,
+    /// Target confidence level for this stratum.
+    pub target_confidence: f64,
+    /// When the debt was first identified (microseconds).
+    pub identified_ts_micros: i64,
+    /// When the debt was last updated (microseconds).
+    pub updated_ts_micros: i64,
+    /// Whether an active experiment is currently addressing this debt.
+    pub experiment_active: bool,
+    /// How much information gain an experiment would provide (estimated).
+    pub estimated_information_gain: f64,
+}
+
+impl IdentifiabilityDebt {
+    /// Whether this debt is severe enough to consider active experimentation.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn warrants_experiment(&self) -> bool {
+        self.severity >= 0.30
+            && self.current_confidence < DECISION_SUFFICIENT_CONFIDENCE
+            && !self.experiment_active
+            && self.estimated_information_gain >= MIN_VOI_SCORE
+    }
+
+    /// Whether this debt has been sufficiently resolved.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn is_resolved(&self) -> bool {
+        self.current_confidence >= self.target_confidence
+            && self.observation_count >= self.required_count
+    }
+}
+
+/// The identifiability-debt ledger: tracks all known evidence gaps.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct DebtLedger {
+    /// All active debt entries.
+    pub entries: Vec<IdentifiabilityDebt>,
+    /// Counter for generating unique debt IDs.
+    pub next_debt_id: u64,
+    /// When the ledger was last scanned/updated.
+    pub last_scan_ts_micros: i64,
+}
+
+impl DebtLedger {
+    /// Create a new empty ledger.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn new() -> Self {
+        Self::default()
+    }
+
+    /// Add a new debt entry to the ledger.
+    pub fn add_debt(
+        &mut self,
+        action_family: String,
+        cohort_key: String,
+        debt_type: DebtType,
+        severity: f64,
+        observation_count: u64,
+        current_confidence: f64,
+        now_micros: i64,
+    ) -> u64 {
+        let debt_id = self.next_debt_id;
+        self.next_debt_id += 1;
+
+        self.entries.push(IdentifiabilityDebt {
+            debt_id,
+            action_family,
+            cohort_key,
+            debt_type,
+            severity: severity.clamp(0.0, 1.0),
+            observation_count,
+            required_count: DECISION_SUFFICIENT_OBS,
+            current_confidence: current_confidence.clamp(0.0, 1.0),
+            target_confidence: DECISION_SUFFICIENT_CONFIDENCE,
+            identified_ts_micros: now_micros,
+            updated_ts_micros: now_micros,
+            experiment_active: false,
+            estimated_information_gain: 0.0,
+        });
+
+        debt_id
+    }
+
+    /// Remove resolved debts from the ledger.
+    pub fn prune_resolved(&mut self) {
+        self.entries.retain(|e| !e.is_resolved());
+    }
+
+    /// Count of debts that warrant experimentation.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn experiment_worthy_count(&self) -> usize {
+        self.entries.iter().filter(|e| e.warrants_experiment()).count()
+    }
+
+    /// Total active (unresolved) debts.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn active_count(&self) -> usize {
+        self.entries.len()
+    }
+
+    /// Debts sorted by severity (highest first).
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn by_severity(&self) -> Vec<&IdentifiabilityDebt> {
+        let mut sorted: Vec<_> = self.entries.iter().collect();
+        sorted.sort_by(|a, b| b.severity.partial_cmp(&a.severity).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
+        sorted
+    }
+}
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Value-of-information scoring
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Input for value-of-information scoring.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct VoIInput {
+    /// The debt entry this experiment would address.
+    pub debt_severity: f64,
+    /// Estimated information gain from the experiment (bits or equivalent).
+    pub estimated_info_gain: f64,
+    /// Current posterior entropy for the relevant stratum.
+    pub posterior_entropy: f64,
+    /// Time since last observation in this stratum (microseconds).
+    pub staleness_micros: i64,
+    /// User-noise cost of the experiment (0.0-1.0).
+    pub noise_cost: f64,
+    /// Fairness burden increment if the experiment runs.
+    pub fairness_cost: f64,
+    /// Tail-risk of the experiment (probability of harmful outcome).
+    pub tail_risk: f64,
+    /// Whether the experiment is reversible.
+    pub is_reversible: bool,
+}
+
+/// Result of value-of-information scoring.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct VoIScore {
+    /// Raw VoI score (information value before costs).
+    pub raw_voi: f64,
+    /// Noise cost penalty.
+    pub noise_penalty: f64,
+    /// Fairness cost penalty.
+    pub fairness_penalty: f64,
+    /// Tail-risk penalty.
+    pub tail_risk_penalty: f64,
+    /// Net VoI score after all penalties.
+    pub net_voi: f64,
+    /// Whether the experiment is justified (net_voi >= MIN_VOI_SCORE).
+    pub justified: bool,
+    /// Reason code for the decision.
+    pub reason: VoIDecision,
+}
+
+/// Decision outcome for a VoI evaluation.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum VoIDecision {
+    /// Experiment is justified: information value exceeds costs.
+    ExperimentJustified,
+    /// Do not experiment: information value is too low.
+    InformationValueTooLow,
+    /// Do not experiment: noise cost is too high relative to benefit.
+    NoiseCostExcessive,
+    /// Do not experiment: fairness budget would be violated.
+    FairnessBudgetViolated,
+    /// Do not experiment: tail risk is unacceptable.
+    TailRiskUnacceptable,
+    /// Do not experiment: action is irreversible.
+    Irreversible,
+    /// Do not experiment: already decision-sufficient.
+    AlreadySufficient,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for VoIDecision {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::ExperimentJustified => write!(f, "experiment_justified"),
+            Self::InformationValueTooLow => write!(f, "information_value_too_low"),
+            Self::NoiseCostExcessive => write!(f, "noise_cost_excessive"),
+            Self::FairnessBudgetViolated => write!(f, "fairness_budget_violated"),
+            Self::TailRiskUnacceptable => write!(f, "tail_risk_unacceptable"),
+            Self::Irreversible => write!(f, "irreversible"),
+            Self::AlreadySufficient => write!(f, "already_sufficient"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Compute the value-of-information score for a candidate experiment.
+///
+/// Returns a `VoIScore` with the net value and decision. "Do not experiment"
+/// is the default outcome — justification requires positive net value.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn score_voi(input: &VoIInput) -> VoIScore {
+    // Irreversible actions never qualify for experimentation.
+    if !input.is_reversible {
+        return VoIScore {
+            raw_voi: 0.0,
+            noise_penalty: 0.0,
+            fairness_penalty: 0.0,
+            tail_risk_penalty: 0.0,
+            net_voi: 0.0,
+            justified: false,
+            reason: VoIDecision::Irreversible,
+        };
+    }
+
+    // Raw VoI: combination of information gain, debt severity, and staleness.
+    // Higher entropy and staleness increase the value of new information.
+    let staleness_factor = (input.staleness_micros as f64 / 600_000_000.0).min(2.0); // cap at 2×
+    let raw_voi = input.estimated_info_gain
+        * input.debt_severity
+        * (1.0 + 0.3 * input.posterior_entropy)
+        * (1.0 + 0.2 * staleness_factor);
+
+    // Cost penalties.
+    let noise_penalty = input.noise_cost * 2.0; // noise is weighted 2× (user trust is precious)
+    let fairness_penalty = input.fairness_cost * 1.5;
+    let tail_risk_penalty = input.tail_risk * 5.0; // tail risk is weighted 5×
+
+    let net_voi = raw_voi - noise_penalty - fairness_penalty - tail_risk_penalty;
+
+    // Decision logic: check each refusal condition.
+    let reason = if raw_voi < MIN_VOI_SCORE {
+        VoIDecision::InformationValueTooLow
+    } else if noise_penalty > raw_voi * MAX_NOISE_TO_VOI_RATIO {
+        VoIDecision::NoiseCostExcessive
+    } else if fairness_penalty > raw_voi * 0.30 {
+        VoIDecision::FairnessBudgetViolated
+    } else if tail_risk_penalty > raw_voi * 0.20 {
+        VoIDecision::TailRiskUnacceptable
+    } else if net_voi >= MIN_VOI_SCORE {
+        VoIDecision::ExperimentJustified
+    } else {
+        VoIDecision::InformationValueTooLow
+    };
+
+    VoIScore {
+        raw_voi,
+        noise_penalty,
+        fairness_penalty,
+        tail_risk_penalty,
+        net_voi,
+        justified: reason == VoIDecision::ExperimentJustified,
+        reason,
+    }
+}
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Experiment eligibility gates
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Result of checking experiment eligibility.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct EligibilityResult {
+    /// Whether the experiment is eligible.
+    pub eligible: bool,
+    /// All gate results (not short-circuited).
+    pub gates: Vec<EligibilityGate>,
+    /// Overall refusal reason (if not eligible).
+    pub refusal: Option<ExperimentRefusalCode>,
+}
+
+/// A single eligibility gate check.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct EligibilityGate {
+    /// Gate name.
+    pub name: String,
+    /// Whether this gate passed.
+    pub passed: bool,
+    /// Description of why it passed or failed.
+    pub description: String,
+}
+
+/// Machine-readable refusal codes for experiment denial.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum ExperimentRefusalCode {
+    /// Action is high-risk (Release, ForceReservation).
+    HighRiskAction,
+    /// Regime is unstable.
+    RegimeUnstable,
+    /// Evidence is contaminated.
+    EvidenceContaminated,
+    /// Budget for this cohort/family is exhausted.
+    BudgetExhausted,
+    /// Normal policy is already decision-sufficient.
+    AlreadySufficient,
+    /// Fairness budget would be violated.
+    FairnessViolation,
+    /// Safe mode is active.
+    SafeModeActive,
+    /// VoI score is too low.
+    VoIInsufficient,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for ExperimentRefusalCode {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::HighRiskAction => write!(f, "high_risk_action"),
+            Self::RegimeUnstable => write!(f, "regime_unstable"),
+            Self::EvidenceContaminated => write!(f, "evidence_contaminated"),
+            Self::BudgetExhausted => write!(f, "budget_exhausted"),
+            Self::AlreadySufficient => write!(f, "already_sufficient"),
+            Self::FairnessViolation => write!(f, "fairness_violation"),
+            Self::SafeModeActive => write!(f, "safe_mode_active"),
+            Self::VoIInsufficient => write!(f, "voi_insufficient"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Context for experiment eligibility checking.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct EligibilityContext {
+    /// The effect kind of the proposed experiment.
+    pub effect_kind: EffectKind,
+    /// Whether the regime is currently stable.
+    pub regime_stable: bool,
+    /// Whether safe mode is active.
+    pub safe_mode_active: bool,
+    /// Fraction of evidence that is trusted quality.
+    pub trusted_evidence_fraction: f64,
+    /// Whether the stratum is already decision-sufficient.
+    pub decision_sufficient: bool,
+    /// Current experiment budget usage for this cohort.
+    pub budget_used: u32,
+    /// Maximum experiments allowed for this cohort.
+    pub budget_max: u32,
+    /// Whether the fairness budget allows this experiment.
+    pub fairness_allows: bool,
+    /// The VoI score for this experiment.
+    pub voi_score: f64,
+}
+
+/// Check all eligibility gates for a candidate experiment.
+///
+/// Returns all gate results (not short-circuited) so the caller can
+/// explain every reason an experiment was denied.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn check_eligibility(ctx: &EligibilityContext) -> EligibilityResult {
+    let mut gates = Vec::new();
+    let mut first_refusal = None;
+
+    // Gate 1: Effect kind must be low or medium risk.
+    let kind_ok = !ctx.effect_kind.is_high_force();
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "risk_level".to_string(),
+        passed: kind_ok,
+        description: if kind_ok {
+            "effect kind is low/medium risk".to_string()
+        } else {
+            "high-risk effects (Release, ForceReservation) cannot be experiments".to_string()
+        },
+    });
+    if !kind_ok && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::HighRiskAction);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 2: Regime must be stable.
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "regime_stability".to_string(),
+        passed: ctx.regime_stable,
+        description: if ctx.regime_stable {
+            "regime is stable".to_string()
+        } else {
+            "experiments are blocked during regime transitions".to_string()
+        },
+    });
+    if !ctx.regime_stable && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::RegimeUnstable);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 3: Safe mode must not be active.
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "safe_mode".to_string(),
+        passed: !ctx.safe_mode_active,
+        description: if !ctx.safe_mode_active {
+            "safe mode inactive".to_string()
+        } else {
+            "experiments are blocked during safe mode".to_string()
+        },
+    });
+    if ctx.safe_mode_active && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::SafeModeActive);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 4: Evidence quality must be sufficient.
+    let evidence_ok = ctx.trusted_evidence_fraction >= 0.50;
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "evidence_quality".to_string(),
+        passed: evidence_ok,
+        description: if evidence_ok {
+            format!("trusted evidence fraction {:.0}% >= 50%", ctx.trusted_evidence_fraction * 100.0)
+        } else {
+            format!("trusted evidence fraction {:.0}% < 50%", ctx.trusted_evidence_fraction * 100.0)
+        },
+    });
+    if !evidence_ok && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::EvidenceContaminated);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 5: Must not already be decision-sufficient.
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "uncertainty_limited".to_string(),
+        passed: !ctx.decision_sufficient,
+        description: if !ctx.decision_sufficient {
+            "stratum is uncertainty-limited, experiments may help".to_string()
+        } else {
+            "stratum is already decision-sufficient, no experiment needed".to_string()
+        },
+    });
+    if ctx.decision_sufficient && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::AlreadySufficient);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 6: Budget must not be exhausted.
+    let budget_ok = ctx.budget_used < ctx.budget_max;
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "experiment_budget".to_string(),
+        passed: budget_ok,
+        description: if budget_ok {
+            format!("budget: {}/{} used", ctx.budget_used, ctx.budget_max)
+        } else {
+            format!("budget exhausted: {}/{}", ctx.budget_used, ctx.budget_max)
+        },
+    });
+    if !budget_ok && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::BudgetExhausted);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 7: Fairness must allow.
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "fairness".to_string(),
+        passed: ctx.fairness_allows,
+        description: if ctx.fairness_allows {
+            "fairness budget allows this experiment".to_string()
+        } else {
+            "experiment would violate fairness constraints".to_string()
+        },
+    });
+    if !ctx.fairness_allows && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::FairnessViolation);
+    }
+
+    // Gate 8: VoI must be sufficient.
+    let voi_ok = ctx.voi_score >= MIN_VOI_SCORE;
+    gates.push(EligibilityGate {
+        name: "voi_threshold".to_string(),
+        passed: voi_ok,
+        description: if voi_ok {
+            format!("VoI score {:.3} >= {MIN_VOI_SCORE:.3}", ctx.voi_score)
+        } else {
+            format!("VoI score {:.3} < {MIN_VOI_SCORE:.3}: not worth experimenting", ctx.voi_score)
+        },
+    });
+    if !voi_ok && first_refusal.is_none() {
+        first_refusal = Some(ExperimentRefusalCode::VoIInsufficient);
+    }
+
+    let eligible = first_refusal.is_none();
+
+    EligibilityResult {
+        eligible,
+        gates,
+        refusal: first_refusal,
+    }
+}

+/// Budget entry for a specific (action family, cohort, time window) triple.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct ExperimentBudget {
+    /// Action family this budget applies to.
+    pub action_family: String,
+    /// Cohort key (e.g., "claude-code:project-x").
+    pub cohort_key: String,
+    /// Maximum experiments in the current window.
+    pub max_experiments: u32,
+    /// Experiments used in the current window.
+    pub used_experiments: u32,
+    /// Window start (microseconds).
+    pub window_start_micros: i64,
+    /// Window duration (microseconds).
+    pub window_duration_micros: i64,
+}
+
+impl ExperimentBudget {
+    /// Whether the budget has capacity for another experiment.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn has_capacity(&self) -> bool {
+        self.used_experiments < self.max_experiments
+    }
+
+    /// Whether the window has expired.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn is_expired(&self, now_micros: i64) -> bool {
+        now_micros - self.window_start_micros > self.window_duration_micros
+    }
+
+    /// Consume one experiment from the budget. Returns false if exhausted.
+    pub fn consume(&mut self) -> bool {
+        if self.has_capacity() {
+            self.used_experiments += 1;
+            true
+        } else {
+            false
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// Reset the budget for a new window.
+    pub fn reset(&mut self, now_micros: i64) {
+        self.used_experiments = 0;
+        self.window_start_micros = now_micros;
+    }
+}
+
+/// Budget table: manages experiment budgets across families and cohorts.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct ExperimentBudgetTable {
+    /// All budget entries, keyed by "family:cohort".
+    pub entries: Vec<ExperimentBudget>,
+    /// Global experiment counter for the current regime.
+    pub global_experiment_count: u64,
+    /// Total actions (for computing experiment fraction).
+    pub global_action_count: u64,
+}
+
+impl ExperimentBudgetTable {
+    /// Create a new empty budget table.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn new() -> Self {
+        Self::default()
+    }
+
+    /// Get or create a budget entry for the given family and cohort.
+    pub fn get_or_create(
+        &mut self,
+        action_family: &str,
+        cohort_key: &str,
+        now_micros: i64,
+    ) -> &mut ExperimentBudget {
+        // Find existing entry.
+        let idx = self.entries.iter().position(|e| {
+            e.action_family == action_family && e.cohort_key == cohort_key
+        });
+
+        if let Some(i) = idx {
+            let entry = &mut self.entries[i];
+            if entry.is_expired(now_micros) {
+                entry.reset(now_micros);
+            }
+            return &mut self.entries[i];
+        }
+
+        // Create new entry.
+        self.entries.push(ExperimentBudget {
+            action_family: action_family.to_string(),
+            cohort_key: cohort_key.to_string(),
+            max_experiments: MAX_EXPERIMENTS_PER_COHORT_PER_HOUR,
+            used_experiments: 0,
+            window_start_micros: now_micros,
+            window_duration_micros: DEFAULT_EXPERIMENT_WINDOW_MICROS,
+        });
+        self.entries.last_mut().unwrap()
+    }
+
+    /// Whether the global experiment fraction is within limits.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn global_fraction_ok(&self) -> bool {
+        if self.global_action_count == 0 {
+            return true;
+        }
+        (self.global_experiment_count as f64 / self.global_action_count as f64)
+            <= MAX_EXPERIMENT_FRACTION_GLOBAL
+    }
+
+    /// Record an experiment being executed.
+    pub fn record_experiment(&mut self) {
+        self.global_experiment_count += 1;
+    }
+
+    /// Record any action being executed (for fraction tracking).
+    pub fn record_action(&mut self) {
+        self.global_action_count += 1;
+    }
+}

+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Experiment record
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// A logged experiment with its motivation, design, and linkage.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct ExperimentRecord {
+    /// Unique experiment ID.
+    pub experiment_id: u64,
+    /// Which debt entry motivated this experiment.
+    pub debt_id: u64,
+    /// The action family being experimented on.
+    pub action_family: String,
+    /// The cohort/stratum being targeted.
+    pub cohort_key: String,
+    /// The effect kind used for the experiment.
+    pub effect_kind: EffectKind,
+    /// The agent targeted by the experiment.
+    pub target_agent: String,
+    /// VoI score that justified the experiment.
+    pub voi_score: f64,
+    /// VoI decision that authorized it.
+    pub voi_decision: VoIDecision,
+    /// Whether the experiment has both control and information value.
+    pub dual_purpose: bool,
+    /// Experience ID linked to this experiment (when available).
+    pub experience_id: Option<u64>,
+    /// Decision ID that generated this experiment.
+    pub decision_id: u64,
+    /// When the experiment was created (microseconds).
+    pub created_ts_micros: i64,
+    /// Status of the experiment.
+    pub status: ExperimentStatus,
+    /// Regime ID during the experiment.
+    pub regime_id: u64,
+}
+
+/// Status of an experiment.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum ExperimentStatus {
+    /// Experiment is planned but not yet executed.
+    Planned,
+    /// Experiment has been dispatched.
+    Dispatched,
+    /// Experiment outcome has been observed.
+    Observed,
+    /// Experiment was cancelled (e.g., regime change, budget exhaustion).
+    Cancelled,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for ExperimentStatus {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::Planned => write!(f, "planned"),
+            Self::Dispatched => write!(f, "dispatched"),
+            Self::Observed => write!(f, "observed"),
+            Self::Cancelled => write!(f, "cancelled"),
+        }
+    }
+}
